code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a written work into a single fluent sentence.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""
    # Simple templates for common predicates
    templates = {
        "author": "{subject} was written by {object}",
        "isbnNumber": "its ISBN number is {object}",
        "followedBy": "it was followed by {object}",
        "precededBy": "it was preceded by {object}",
        "language": "it is written in {object}",
        "mediaType": "its media type is {object}",
        "numberOfPages": "it has {object} pages",
        "country": "it was published in {object}",
        "publisher": "its publisher is {object}",
        "genre": "it belongs to the {object} genre",
        "literaryGenre": "its literary genre is {object}",
        "releaseDate": "it was released on {object}",
        "birthDate": "the author was born on {object}",
        # Additional predicates can be added here
    }
    clauses = []
    for t in triples:
        tmpl = templates.get(t.predicate)
        if tmpl:
            clause = tmpl.format(subject=t.subject, object=t.object)
        else:
            clause = f"{t.subject} {t.predicate} {t.object}"
        clauses.append(clause)
    # Combine clauses into one sentence
    if len(clauses) == 1:
        sentence = clauses[0]
    else:
        sentence = ", ".join(clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + clauses[-1]
    # Ensure proper capitalization and punctuation
    return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:] + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
